title: NOC 12104 – Employment Insurance and Revenue Officers | GTR Immigration
description: Employment Insurance and Revenue Officers are central to public administration, taxation, and benefits delivery across Canada. Under NOC 12104, this occupation continues to see steady hiring growth in federal, provincial, and municipal institutions—creating realistic opportunities for skilled newcomers seeking Permanent Residence (PR).

classification.teer: 2 – Occupations usually requiring a college diploma or apprenticeship training of two or more years; or supervisory occupations

duties: Audit accounting records to verify taxable income and exemptions
Review organizational accounting systems and controls
Advise businesses on tax reporting methods
Prepare court briefs and assist in investigations
Support enforcement actions related to tax compliance
employment_requirements: A bachelor’s degree or college diploma
Several years of regulatory or administrative experience
Completion of specialized government training programs
job_outlook: Between 2022 and 2031, Canada is projected to create 13,800 new positions for Employment Insurance and Revenue Officers, while approximately 15,200 job seekers are expected to enter the field. Strategic province selection and a strong immigration plan can significantly improve prospects for overseas applicants.
benefits: Assess eligibility for Employment Insurance (EI), CPP, and OAS benefits
Detect and follow up on suspected fraud or misuse
additional_information: Employment Insurance and Revenue Officers are actively recruited across government departments and regulatory agencies.

Stable public-sector hiringStrong post-pandemic administrative demandEligibility for Express Entry and Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs)Government-supported career progressionAnnual earnings typically ranging from $48,200 to $87,800
